note: Maker-authored assessment of the AI-UX patterns the Fieldwork agentic composition (the /agentic-ui-study screen) ships TODAY — not agent output. Every `how` names a real, present affordance (see system/agentic-study.mjs); every `cite` is verified against its primary source. Screen/flow-level rubric; a screen's full rubric is this ∪ the `aiPatterns` its components carry (kb-format §ComponentSpec). #41.

patterns.how: the reader picks from a bounded set of questions — never a blank prompt — via the study's 'Pick a question' tablist; the bounded set is itself a statement of what the feature can do
patterns.cite: Microsoft Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction — G1 'Make clear what the system can do', G7 'Support efficient invocation'; Google PAIR — User Needs + Defining Success

patterns.how: the agent's proposed composition is shown first — slot preview plus the raw {name, props, children} — before the reader acts; every adjustment edits a deep-cloned working copy, never the committed proposal
patterns.cite: Nielsen — Visibility of system status (#1); Microsoft Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction — G9 'Support efficient correction'

patterns.how: 'Reset to the agent's proposal' returns to the committed state; tone, remove and reorder are all reversible; reaching past the vocabulary refuses non-destructively — the control reverts and the working copy is untouched
patterns.cite: Nielsen — User control and freedom (#3; undo/redo and 'emergency exit'); Microsoft Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction — G17 'Provide global controls'

patterns.how: links the committed real-run PIV trace, dumps the raw composition JSON live, and names the exact refused out-of-vocabulary path verbatim when a probe is rejected
patterns.cite: Microsoft Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction — G11 'Make clear why the system did what it did'; Google PAIR — Explainability + Trust

patterns.how: the composition is labeled real, curated agent output ('Real run, curated for length') and stated to be precomputed at build time — there is no live model call at view time
patterns.cite: Microsoft Guidelines for Human-AI Interaction — G1 'Make clear what the system can do', G2 'Make clear how well the system can do what it can do'; Google PAIR — Explainability + Trust (transparency about what ran — PAIR does not phrase this as 'label AI content', so it is not cited that way)

patterns.how: each proposed view answers a real operational dispatch question ('what's the operational state of the board', 'where is SLA-breach risk concentrated') with figures computed from the real Fieldwork fixtures — open · SLA-at-risk · overdue · needs-assignment — so the agent's output is decision support grounded in the actual board state
patterns.cite: Google PAIR — User Needs + Defining Success (ground the feature in a real user need and define success by it)
